aspects.title: Stop
aspects.recognition: One steady red light on a Ks signal with main-signal function.
playerAction: Bring the train to a stand before the signal.
Do not pass without an appropriate authority or subsidiary indication.

pzb.summary: Passing an active 2000 Hz magnet without authority causes an emergency brake application.
notes: The magnet state and permitted passing procedure depend on the operational situation and any subsidiary indication.

aspects.title: Proceed, expect proceed
aspects.meaning: Proceed. The signal does not announce a stop at the next main signal. Any displayed speed indicator still applies.
playerAction: Proceed at the speed permitted by the timetable, cab signalling and any Zs 3 indication.
Check for a Zs 3v indication announcing a lower speed at the next signal.

pzb.summary: The plain proceed aspect normally requires no PZB acknowledgement.
notes: Other signs or restrictions at the same location may independently require action.

aspects.title: Proceed, expect a lower indicated speed
aspects.recognition: One flashing green light, used with a Zs 3v speed pre-indicator.
aspects.meaning: Proceed. Expect the lower speed shown by Zs 3v from the next signal or defined location.
playerAction: Proceed at the currently permitted speed.
Read the yellow Zs 3v numeral and begin braking so the announced speed is reached at its required point.
Acknowledge PZB when the associated 1000 Hz influence is active.

pzb.summary: A restrictive speed announcement may be protected by an active 1000 Hz magnet and require acknowledgement.
notes: The database must not infer magnet activation solely from lamp colour; route evidence should record the simulated implementation.

aspects.title: Proceed, expect stop
aspects.meaning: Proceed and expect the next main signal to show stop.
playerAction: Acknowledge PZB when the 1000 Hz influence is active.
Begin braking and approach the next main signal prepared to stop before it.
Observe any speed indication displayed with the signal.

pzb.summary: The warning is normally associated with a 1000 Hz influence requiring acknowledgement when active.
notes: PZB monitoring depends on train type and the simulated magnet state.
